projects.title: Eurotech
projects.subtitle: Modernizing identity for a leading national office furniture company.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, Environments + Trade Shows
projects.description:
Eurotech, a leading United States-based office seating company, has prided itself for over 40 years on products with superior comfort, state-of-the-art technology, and cutting-edge materials. Providing stylish, durable products for any office and budget, and boasting exclusive partnerships with Tempur-Pedic® and Outlast® space-grade technology, Eurotech needed a contemporary brand identity system reflective of the best-in-class, innovative office seating it offers. 

MHCG designed a new identity framework for Eurotech, based on the company’s existing logo, which unifies all of the company’s distinct brands, partnerships, and product lines under a streamlined vision, and sets up frameworks for the brand’s growth and expansion. The updated brand identity was applied to a suite of corporate collateral and marketing materials, as well as a dynamic website transformation.

MHCG also set forth the industry launch of Eurotech’s POWERFIT, the first-of-its-kind chair with Bionic Spine technology, a Bluetooth smart seat cushion, and mobile app integration. We created custom environmental graphics to introduce POWERFIT across every surface of Eurotech’s 2,000 sq. ft. NeoCon tradeshow booth, for the world’s leading commercial design industry event.

projects.title: Trish McEvoy Cosmetics
projects.subtitle: Driving a strategic makeover for a luxury cosmetics and skincare brand.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Digital + Web Development
projects.description:
Trish McEvoy has long been a household name in luxury cosmetics and skincare, with a tradition of innovation within the beauty industry and a 50-year legacy of results. As in-store retail plummeted through the early stages of the COVID-19 pandemic, consumer willingness to trade in-store experiences for online, tech-savvy solutions became paramount. MHCG began a rapid digital transformation to help the brand adapt to a shifting market, maintain their cutting-edge relevance, and appealing to a new generation of consumers. From state-of-the-art beauty tech, to upgraded client services, device-compatible email marketing, company-wide brand standards, and an overhaul of outdated digital marketing best practices, our worked repositioned the Trish McEvoy brand for the modern consumer.

MHCG used an integrated approach to procure and implement leading virtual reality sales tools, including the latest in virtual try-on (VTO) technology. MHCG enhanced and cleaned up the flagship website experience through extensive auditing and strategic recommendations for user experience, site speed performance optimization, calendar scheduling, and mobile responsiveness. Our work also included deployment, QA-testing, and agent training processes for a live-chat customer service software integration, as well as expert video chat and on-demand integrated beauty consultations to connect with consumers.

BRAND RE-INTRODUCTION CAMPAIGN
In tandem with the facilitation of technology upgrades, MHCG’s strategic recommendations encouraged the brand to dial up its core values. Our updated go-forward strategy urged the brand to bring fresh photography, layouts, iconography to the forefront, and begin incorporating race-inclusive, age-inclusive, size-inclusive, and flaws-inclusive imagery throughout all marketing channels. The result is a refreshed legacy beauty brand that appeals to multi-generational consumers and is adapted for the digital age.

While our work with Trish McEvoy is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: JPMorgan
projects.subtitle: Defining identity for the International Council, the bank’s premier annual event.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Premiums + Promotions, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
The J.P. Morgan Chase International Council is J.P. Morgan Chase’s premier annual event. The Council is comprised of 250 members from 19 nations, and convenes annually in a leading world city to gain perspectives on economic, political and social trends in key regions and countries of the world. International Council members provide advice to the senior management of J.P. Morgan Chase on these and other issues affecting its clients and business.

Since 2010, MHCG has been spearheading the development of branding, as well as print and online creative for the annual event. MHCG has received numerous awards for its work, specifically in brand development and strategy.

While our work with J.P. Morgan Chase is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: Usher URVIP Experience
projects.subtitle: Debuting a premium VIP experience for Usher’s Las Vegas residency.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, Environments + Trade Shows, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
Global megastar and eight-time Grammy Award-winning R&B/pop star, Usher, began his first, one-of-a-kind residency at Caesars Palace, Las Vegas in Summer 2021. To shock and delight mega-fans with an exclusive, premium immersive VIP experience that re-defines fan engagement, hospitality, and entertainment, MHCG and partners developed the ultimate secret VIP fan package, URVIP.

Utilizing QR codes through secret VIP guest touchpoints, trailblazing AR/VR technology, and best-in-class hospitality, we offered mega-fans the ultimate once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to experience Usher throughout their Las Vegas stay.

MHCG created a crest-like emblem to mark the experiential and custom-curated URVIP brand experience. The acronym URVIP plays both with Usher’s initials (Usher Raymond IV) and the concept, “You Are VIP.” From the welcome package to the custom, swag-filled attaché cases, personal URVIP concierges, URVIP all-access backstage passes, and more, this insider brand alerts VIPs to exclusive experiences throughout retail, spa, dining, and hotel touchpoints. If you know, you know. Using exclusive app technology, guests know QR codes with the URVIP brand unlock secret augmented reality experiences with the musician and URVIP-exclusive brand partnerships with premium stores and restaurants.

“This VIP experience is me giving it back to my fans”
 

Our work with Usher’s Caesars Palace residency also included custom-branded environmental experiences within the performance venue including UshBucks cash prize machines, money blowers, branded collaboration graphics, and 80-foot digital display screens.

Returning home, URVIP guests also received a custom, commemorative display with one final personalized AR message from Usher, and thank you note.

projects.title: Middle East Media Research Institute
projects.subtitle: MEMRI: Shifting College Students’ Perspectives on the Hamas Conflict
projects.services: Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content
projects.description:
MEMRI, The Middle East Media Research Institute, enlisted MHCG to design and execute a social action campaign targeting U.S. college campuses. The campaign featured videos and translations of Hamas leadership’s statements on the U.S., women, and LGBTQ+ issues, providing students with an unbiased perspective on the complex Middle East conflict.

To maximize impact, MHCG handled both the creative direction and media buying, securing back cover placements in college newspapers at institutions like the University of Pennsylvania, UCLA, UC Berkeley, and Harvard. In total, 219 college campuses were targeted, generating millions of impressions. The campaign’s messaging emphasized the complexity of truth and encouraged students to explore MEMRI’s trusted, nonpartisan translations via QR codes, bus ads, and digital platforms. In doing so, MHCG also helped build MEMRI’s brand equity among students and faculty across these key campuses.

As a nonprofit, MEMRI bridges the language gap between the West and the Middle East by providing timely translations of Arabic media and original analysis of critical regional trends.

Reach: College newspapers in the U.S. reach an audience of over 20 million students. Many of these students rely on their school newspapers for campus news, events, and social issues, making college newspapers a highly effective platform for targeted campaigns.

projects.title: MAGEN DAVID ADOM
projects.subtitle: Designing Clarity in Crisis for Israel's Global Emergency System
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Social Content, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
During one of the most challenging periods in Israel’s modern history, MHCG was engaged to lead a comprehensive global rebrand for Magen David Adom — modernizing its identity for clarity, cohesion, and scale, and aligning its worldwide network of friends organizations.
As Israel’s national emergency medical services organization, Magen David Adom defines and operates the country’s ambulance system, blood services, and emergency response infrastructure. In the wake of October 7th, the need for a unified, globally recognizable brand became even more urgent — both to support operational clarity at home and to mobilize international philanthropic support abroad. The strategic outcome of this work formally defined the organization as Israel’s Emergency Services System, bringing precision and global understanding to its role.
MHCG’s engagement defined a full-spectrum brand transformation: a modernized visual identity, updated emblems and insignia, redesigned uniforms, refreshed fleet graphics, global campaign assets for post–October 7th fundraising, and system-wide alignment across international partner organizations.
Beyond identity, the work extended into experience and engagement — including a traveling memorial installation concept, integrations and updates to MDA’s public safety app, and a collaborative installation with Israeli street artist Dede Bandaid at Miami Art Week — creating a bold, culturally resonant platform that connected mission, memory, and modern design.
The result was a stronger, more unified global brand system — one that reflects the organization’s scale, urgency, and humanitarian responsibility, while ensuring clarity for audiences worldwide.

projects.title: Optifino
projects.subtitle: Building a visionary brand experience for a breakthrough insurtech company.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
Optifino (previously FamFi) — a one-of-a-kind, client-centric life insurance fintech company — engaged MHCG to build lasting value by developing a new name, comprehensive brand identity, and positioning strategy. Optifino is a revolutionary solution designed to bridge the gap between life insurance and investing, by delivering flexible, cutting-edge life insurance products to high-net-worth investors through an innovative technology platform.

Through precise creative strategy and brand development, MHCG helped Optifino articulate its vision of elevating life insurance solutions to an artform, transforming a symphony of possibilities into a curated portfolio of policies to suit clients’ unique vision and needs in real-time. MHCG developed a distinct name and brand identity with sophisticated B2B and B2C appeal, to target the RIA and wealth planning professional community, as well as savvy high-net-worth investors.

projects.title: Aaron Matthew SIDS Research Guild
projects.subtitle: Racing to end the fight against Sudden Infant Death Syndrome.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Print, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
MHCG developed a new donor information brochure for the Aaron Matthew SIDS Research Guild, a nonprofit established to fund much-need research about sudden infant death syndrome (SIDS), in partnership with Seattle Children’s Hospital Integrative Brain Research Institute, Microsoft’s leading data scientists, and various medical research centers across the globe.

The guild was founded by Heather Kahan and John B. Kahan, Chief Data and Analytics Officer at Microsoft, in honor of their son, Aaron Matthew Kahan, who died of SIDS just days after his birth in 2003. SIDS is the sudden and unexplained death of an infant younger than one year old, and is the leading cause of death among infants one month to one year old in the United States. There has been little to no improvement in the rate of death since the mid-90’s. SIDS strikes without warning and leaves few clues about its causes, which are still unknown.

The guild’s revolutionary model for innovation brings together world-class data scientists and leading medical researchers to create new hypotheses about SIDS, by leveraging big data and AI to look at SIDS from all sides: epidemiology, pathology, genetics, physiology, and behavioral change. This powerful research vehicle has led to critical breakthroughs in biomedical research to help understand and decode SIDS. The guild is also embarking on its most promising research effort to date — building the first cloud-based genetic database dedicated to SIDS and infant mortality.

MHCG worked with the guild to clarify their message and emphasize the robust connection between its model for innovation and the critical breakthroughs it has made in SIDS research. MHCG created custom information graphics and iconography to show the scope of the story and reveal the necessity for funding this crucial research.

Although significant strides have been made and the guild researchers are closer than ever to understanding SIDS, there is still much work to be done. MHCG’s efforts to support the Aaron Matthew SIDS Research Guild and distill its message will help spread vital awareness about sudden infant death syndrome and the research being done to bring us closer to a world without SIDS.

projects.title: The One Toronto
projects.subtitle: Building anticipation for Canada’s tallest superstructure in the heart of Toronto.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Social Content, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
The One Toronto by Mizrahi Developments and world-renowned architects Foster + Partners represents limitless elegance, a mesmerizing glass curtain rising 308 meters above the beating heart of Toronto’s busiest city streets. The One redefines the standard of luxury with 85 storeys of exceptionally-crafted high-rise residences, a flagship Hyatt Andaz hotel, cutting-edge dining, personalized five-star concierge services, world-class in-room spa services, a state-of-the-art fitness center, and high-end retail shopping in the heart of Canada’s leading luxury epicenter. The Limited Collection of high-rise suites and penthouses at The One offers complete customization high above the city with truly adaptable interior floor plans, highest-quality finishes, unprecedented panoramic floor-to-ceiling views, and exterior inter-connecting gardens. Positioned at the intersection of busy Yonge and Bloor streets, The One sits at the urban nexus of Toronto’s downtown core and fashionable Yorkville neighbourhoods, bringing the best of Toronto to your doorstep.

For more information about our work with The One Toronto, please contact us.

projects.title: Centerbridge Partners
projects.subtitle: Defining sustainable value creation and ESG commitment for a leading private investment firm.
projects.services: Direct Marketing, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
Centerbridge Partners, a top private investment management firm based in New York City and London, engaged MHCG to design the firm’s annual Sustainable Value Creation Reports, as well as various LP corporate communications. Working closely with the firm, MHCG leveraged high impact graphics with Centerbridge’s clean visual identity to better communicate the firm’s commitment to Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) considerations and Inclusion and Diversity (I&D) values.

While our work with Centerbridge Partners is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: Coinbase
projects.subtitle: Throwing it back 90’s style for a leading cryptocurrency company’s holiday event.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Direct Marketing, Premiums + Promotions, Environments + Trade Shows
projects.description:
Coinbase, a leading crytpocurrency exchange platform, wanted to engage its global team with a surprise, throwback end-of-year holiday talent show event takeover of the routine weekly town hall meeting. MHCG created an 8-bit style event logo for the Coinbase “Talent Hall,” as well as all of the custom virtual-environmental graphics for the event. Inspired by the style of the mid-90’s macOS 5.5 interface, MHCG built out a pixelated vaporwave fantasy environment as the backdrop for the virtual talent show. MHCG also designed and developed a 3D-printed “Coiny” award for the winning acts.

While our work with Coinbase is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: Special Olympics
projects.subtitle: Sparking a global inclusion revolution to celebrate 50 years of transformation.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation
projects.description:
Amid their 50th anniversary, MHCG worked with Special Olympics on a multi-prong go-forward strategy, which laid out executional requirements, expansions, and channels for launch, and set forth recommendations for post-launch expansion to carry the launch to larger national and international arenas.

While our work with Special Olympics is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Feel free to contact us for more information.

projects.title: Hotel on Rivington
projects.subtitle: Branding a high-touch boutique hotel on NYC’s Lower East Side.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
The Hotel on Rivington is a premier destination in the revitalized Lower East Side. The hotel is an urban oasis with spacious accommodations that are unrivaled in New York City, as well as a sleek modern design and stunning skyline views. After nearly 15 years, HOR needed a creative partner who could simultaneously provide both guidance and vision for new opportunities among changing consumer segments and lifestyle trends. From branding to print, to digital to collateral, MHCG envisioned a core organizing strategy to maximize the legacy of the hotel. This strategy included targets and segments where it leveraged strongly, all while giving loyal customers differentiated options for their travel needs. We also identified opportunities to give HOR a new array of pioneer experiences and uncover new audiences in an increasingly competitive market.

MHCG designers created a series of principles that informed every part of the hotel’s next-generation experience—things like a to-go concept for breakfast and online check-in experience to a hotel with a high-touch, well-designed user experience.

projects.title: Polaris Medical Resorts
projects.subtitle: Transforming perceptions for a portfolio of elite post-hospital care and rehab facilities.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Print, Social Content, Environments + Trade Shows
projects.description:
Polaris is an integrated system of elite sub-acute care facilities throughout Missouri state. MHCG was approached by its capital management company to strategically rebrand the portfolio of 18+ facilities. Initially the facilities worked under various brand names, each with its own distinct yet uninspiring brand image.

Our mandate was to transform the current portfolio into premier, first-choice facilities for all MO in-patient, sub-acute health needs by defining a striking brand system under a single name. Distinct tiers denote hospitality- and amenity-based facility levels. The result is a flexible brand system that showcases Polaris facilities’ cutting-edge amenities, holistic treatment, and elite care.

projects.title: South Africa Tourism
projects.subtitle: Leaving ordinary behind with South Africa Tourism.
projects.services: Print, Direct Marketing

projects.title: Noitacov Resorts & Spa
projects.subtitle: Defining a brand for a luxury golf resort and spa with world-class amenities.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description: Noitacov Resorts & Spa is a premier, boutique golf resort and spa, and the model for the modern getaway. While our work with Noitacov is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: Associated
projects.subtitle: Redefining quality for the largest supermarket chain in the NY metro area.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture
projects.description: In a landscape with fresher new entrants like Whole Foods and Fairway, Associated had to ‘up’ its retail presentation game. MHCG recognized a significant need for a new- and comprehensive- visual identification system. We redesigned their logo and signage, revamped their branding colors, revised and updated their marketing materials, all without sacrificing the existing equity elements that had resonated positively among their loyal core demographic. This revamp signaled a change to the market at large — that Associated was in fine fighting form — and reassured loyalists that Associated hadn’t abandoned their core customers.

projects.title: Humankind Investments
projects.subtitle: Championing ESG values for a human-centered investment firm and ETF launch.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
Humankind Investments is a new kind of investment company, focused on social responsibility, ESG values, total corporate impact, and investing for the future of humanity. Just thirty days prior to the launch of its US Stock ETF, Humankind engaged MHCG to revitalize its existing brand, produce an inspiring brand video to communicate values, and develop a series of ads to promote the company and its ETF via social channels.

In a saturated market of investment products, MHCG helped Humankind distill the message that all investment decisions have an impact, and many have unintended consequences of funding systems that have negative consequences for the planet and for humanity.

MHCG developed a contemporary brand system to upgrade and modernize the company’s existing logo. We created impactful composite images to juxtapose typical investment imagery with their positive, or negative, real-world outcomes.

projects.title: Book of Jewish Knowledge
projects.subtitle: Promoting a high-end luxury coffee table and reference book.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Environments + Trade Shows
projects.description: For more information about our work with the Book of Jewish Knowledge, please contact us.

projects.title: Apexon
projects.subtitle: Launching a full-scale rebrand for an award-winning IT solutions provider.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
Technosoft Corporation, an award-winning provider of Information Technology solutions engaged by over 35 Fortune 500 companies, was gearing up to announce the launch of its new brand and expanded scope of capabilities. Technosoft was its former name; Apexon would be its new DNA going forward. MHCG Inc. partnered with Apexon to drive its rebranding effort across all media.

Technosoft Corporation has long been ranked as one of the top service providers of IT management solutions worldwide. To compete in the changing technological climate and to better service the needs of clients, Technosoft expanded its scope of capabilities to a more comprehensive offering of IT and SAP solutions. As a result, they underwent a digital transformation of their own. The existing brand was stale and lacked foundational elements, including brand guidelines and mission statement. The existing digital ecosystem was highly fragmented, and segmented key portions of information throughout text-dense tabs.

MHCG Inc. assessed strengths, weaknesses and key opportunities for brand positioning and foundational strategy based on an extensive discovery phase and onsite interviews with corporate leadership. We determined that Apexon needed a driving, energetic brand position to propel Technosoft’s trusted legacy into the future. We also constructed strategic naming, messaging, and creative development that blended components of the existing brand and color scheme with new typography. The result is an integrated, contemporary identity system across all media — from print and digital, sales presentations, and exterior signage, to iconography, promotional materials, and corporate event environments.

Now Apexon has the tools they needed to bring their brand to life. To launch this disruptive new force, MHCG Inc. worked diligently with internal marketing teams to ensure they could implement the new brand strategy and communications beyond the scope of our engagement. These teams were thoroughly pleased with Apexon’s dynamic new look and eager to utilize the new marketing communications for global engagement.

projects.title: Camp Simcha
projects.subtitle: Inspiring hope and perseverance at a summer camp for critically-ill children.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
Each summer, 430 seriously-ill children are treated to an unforgettable, medically-supervised overnight camp experience at Camp Simcha — for children battling cancer and other blood disorders — or at Camp Simcha Special — for children with chronic illnesses. In these experiences, the only kosher overnight camps for sick kids and teens, campers trade hospital rooms and doctors’ offices for summers of fun and friendship, building confidence and courage to persevere through adversity.

MHCG Inc. worked with Camp Simcha to develop a lively and energizing new brand identity to inspire hearts and translate the camp’s critical mission. We also developed a series of high-quality renderings to help convey the camp’s proposed expansion and solicit the necessary funding to bring the project to life.

projects.title: National Jewish Health
projects.subtitle: Sustaining vital research and treatment through a record-breaking hospital fundraiser gala.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
National Jewish Health, the leading respiratory hospital in the nation, holds an annual Beaux Arts Ball to garner support for their continuing research and treatment of lung, heart, and immune-related diseases — including COVID-19. For the 2021 Beaux Arts Ball, thousands of supporters across the US and around the world gathered virtually to celebrate the hospital and to raise a record-breaking $3 million. MHCG collaborated with National Jewish Health on a brand identity for the virtual event, which was based on and featured Broadway’s original cast of “The Prom,” and was emceed by former Denver Broncos star, Reggie Rivers.

MHCG also developed a suite of marketing materials for the event, including the event registration and donation website, direct marketing mailers, social media content, and key graphics.

projects.title: Futerfas Law
projects.subtitle: Highlighting a leading law firm’s superior track record.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development
projects.description: Alan Futerfas is a criminal defense lawyer, most recognized for his work defending Donald Trump, Jr. and the Trump Organization. Futerfas needed an upgraded brand and website to usher in the most high-profile case of the firm’s 30+ year litigation history. MHCG developed a simplified and high-impact logo to transition the firm to Futerfas Law from The Law Offices of Alan S. Futerfas, and fostered a sophisticated and high-impact website interface to highlight the firm’s attorneys and capabilities.

projects.title: The Global Innovation Center
projects.subtitle: Shaping the future of global humanitarian impact.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Print, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
JDC’s century of transformational work in over 70 countries around the world provides vital aid to vulnerable and fledgling communities in need, responds to global emergencies, and develops innovative solutions to complex social problems around the globe.

MHCG was asked to provide concept development for a new vision of JDC’s 100-thousand square foot global operations campus in Jerusalem — the hub of humanitarian innovation from which JDC’s global programs are deployed. Nestled in the heart of Jerusalem and surrounded by government offices and institutions of paramount national importance, the revitalized campus would support the organization’s award-winning humanitarian response efforts, and create an environment that supports gifted innovation, efficiency, thought leadership, and impact.

MHCG’s concept development, 3D photorealistic renderings, and presentation designs have been implemented in targeted, “proof of concept” donor presentations on JDC’s quest to find a lead donor to finance the project. The opportunity for philanthropic giving is accompanied by the honor of having the donor’s name engraved into the stone of the Jerusalem skyline.

projects.title: Sphera Funds Management
projects.subtitle: Defining an elevated look for global healthcare and biotech investment funds.
projects.services: Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description: For more information about our work with Sphera Funds Management, please contact us.

projects.title: Copia
projects.subtitle: Illuminating the first social e-reading and commerce experience.
projects.services: Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description: Copia is one of the largest, independent, full-feature digital content platforms in the education space, providing a comprehensive and customizable instructional solution that meets the needs of educators across Higher Education and K-12 markets. As part of a series of funding initiatives and the process of establishing partner priorities, MHCG drove the pitch strategy and developed presentation materials. MHCG created all charts, graphs, and graphical assets to convey Copia’s footprint in the market, and demonstrate its critical value of providing cohesive, immediate, immersive, and interactive educational experiences designed with the student and educator in mind.

projects.title: Lincoln Avenue Capital
projects.subtitle: Showcasing intersectional value for a mission-driven, impact-focused real estate developer.
projects.services: Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
Lincoln Avenue Capital, a real estate developer investing in nationwide affordable housing to strengthen communities and tackle the affordable housing crisis, approached MHCG to create an inspiring impact report. Our focus was centered on communicating the remarkable strides they’ve made toward delivering impactful financial, social, and environmental returns.

MHCG produced an engaging report filled with bold photography, custom information graphics and iconography, and bright colors to underscore the creative measures the company is taking to ensure its investments are good for communities, the environment, and the bottom line.

projects.title: Platinum Cleaning
projects.subtitle: Elevating a nationwide office cleaning and facilities services provider.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
Platinum is a national cleaning and facilities services provider committed to first-tier service, that delivers deep levels of industry specialization and individualized attention. They value consistent quality, innovative cleaning technologies, and safety for the welfare of their employees and clients, but were having trouble communicating their values to their customers.

MHCG collaborated with Platinum on a new brand identity and strategy that would immediately convey the company’s values and personalized service, along with a revitalized website, corporate collateral suite, and set of marketing materials to carry the identity through.

projects.title: Female Sexual Health Portal
projects.subtitle: Driving innovation and destigmatizing women’s sexual health in Canada.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
The Female Sexual Health Portal, supported by the Women’s College Innovation Fund and in partnership with Women’s College Hospital and the University of Toronto, was originated as a resource for patients and healthcare providers seeking more information on the causes, evaluation, and treatment of female sexual dysfunction. Due to its long-regarded history as a taboo topic, there’s a general lack of information surrounding female health and the prevalence of common dysfunctions. Women’s College Hospital sought a partner to help bridge that information gap through an accessible, female-forward portal with facts and statistics to emphasize the prevalent nature of these issues.

MHCG developed the visual aesthetic and website interface to appeal to a spectrum of modern, multi-faceted female and non-binary patients. Promoting the concept that no one should have to live with low desire, lack of orgasm, sexual pain, and low arousal without the information and resources necessary to get help, understand the dysfunction, and relate their experience to others experiencing like symptoms. MHCG also developed a logo for the portal which combines the forms of the Canadian maple leaf and female anatomy to create a warm and inviting brand.

projects.title: JDC
projects.subtitle: Amplifying the dire need for humanitarian relief in the Former Soviet Union.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
The American Jewish Joint Distribution Committee (JDC), a relief organization that provides aid to Jewish populations around the globe, looked to MHCG to help raise funds to support the world’s poorest Jews, who without JDC would be living on just $2 per day.

For 25 years, JDC had operated the largest-scale Jewish humanitarian effort in the former Soviet Union since WWII — bringing vital relief to the poorest Jewish seniors in the world by providing food, medicine, medical care, homecare, rent subsidies, and winter relief to temper the brutal cold. MHCG partnered with JDC to reinvigorate public awareness for the cause and engage large scale donation for the FSU initiative across media channels.

Our work with JDC also includes initiatives for Camp Szarvas and the Global Innovation Center in Jerusalem, Israel.

projects.title: Nariman House
projects.subtitle: Memorializing the site of a landmark terrorist attack in Mumbai, India.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Print
projects.description:
From November 24 to November 29, 2008, terrorists spent nearly one hundred hours in a relentless mass killing in the world’s fifth most populous city — Mumbai, India.

This work showcases the conceptual design, brand identity, and framework for the development of a memorial and museum on the upper floors of the building to honor those who perished at Nariman House and across Mumbai on those devastating days in late 2008. May their memory be a blessing.

projects.title: Reliant Safety
projects.subtitle: Building an identity for a leading virtual enterprise security solutions provider.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print
projects.description:
Reliant Safety has grown to become one of the largest virtual guard companies in the United States. In tandem with Reliant Realty, their partner real estate investment arm specializing in affordable housing property management and compliance, Reliant Safety offers advanced building management and virtual security solutions for full situational awareness and peace of mind. 

MHCG rebranded Reliant Safety for a more streamlined and unified look and feel — applying the upgraded identity to a robust suite of corporate collateral and sales and marketing materials. Our partnership extended across a wide range of print and digital touchpoints, including a transformative website development overhaul with security and hosting, to convey the depth of their results and experience.

projects.title: Olami
projects.subtitle: Reinventing the power of commitment for a global community of organizations.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Print, Direct Marketing, Environments + Trade Shows, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
Olami needed to reinvent themselves, starting with a name change. The company — which has served thousands of young Jewish professionals with the goal of increasing Jewish identity worldwide — was searching for a new brand image, website development, corporate collateral, and experiential event marketing, so the opportunity was huge.

Encompassing a multitude of diverse projects and touchpoints, this foundation services 200+ locations worldwide, including many of the top universities in North America. With a commitment to innovation, high-quality standards and a growing global reach Olami, continues to drive confidently toward a more unified future with greater impact.

projects.title: Treeco
projects.subtitle: Finding a fresh look and feel for a leading real estate company.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Print, Environments + Trade Shows, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description: MHCG advanced a full rebrand for Treeco, a leading real estate development. Investment, and management company with properties throughout the Northeast corridor. MHCG developed a fresh, dynamic brand identity to convey Treeco’s commitment to community revitalization and infinite possibilities, complete with wayfinding environmental signage, corporate collateral, and tradeshow graphics. The existing branding was dated, lacked cohesion, and failed to communicate the company’s contemporary focus on urban revitalization projects. The new brand positioning we developed is friendly, pragmatic, and approachable, focusing on Treeco’s forward-looking perspective and mission to deliver everyday shopping to everyone.

projects.title: Camp Szarvas
projects.subtitle: Renewing lasting memories of camp and comradery, 100 miles outside of Budapest in Szarvas, Hungary.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Print, Direct Marketing, Premiums + Promotions, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description: Camp Szarvas, the JDC-Lauder International Jewish Youth Camp nestled in the Hungarian countryside, draws over 1600 campers from 25+ countries every summer. Founded by JDC and The Ronald S. Lauder Foundation in 1990 as a safe place of Jewish connection for those who suffered under Communism, the camp has drawn 25,000+ campers over the past 28 years and has transformed thousands of young Jews around the globe into leaders. MHCG created a series of stakeholder communications and 3D visualizations for a large-scale capital campaign, as well as the first edition of Szarvas magazine and a high-touch direct marketing donor kit.

projects.title: Kaplan
projects.subtitle: Demonstrating the power of an education’s impact.
projects.services: Digital + Web Development, Print
projects.description: ​Kaplan is one of the world’s largest and most diverse education providers. Throughout their more than 75-year history, Kaplan has been a beacon for expanding educational access and a leader in instructional innovation. MHCG worked with Kaplan to seamlessly translate their 2014/2015 annual report across print and digital media.

projects.title: Raynor Gaming
projects.subtitle: Launching an official gaming partnership with the NBA 2K League.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Digital + Web Development, Print, Environments + Trade Shows, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
Raynor Gaming is a direct-to-consumer Esports seating company, and the official gaming chair partner of the NBA 2K League. Raynor tapped MHCG to create custom chair designs for the exclusive NBA 2K partnership, for all 21 teams in the league: 76ers GC, Blazer5 Gaming, Bucks Gaming, Cavs Legion GC, Celtics Crossover Gaming, Grizz Gaming, Hawks Talon GC, Heat Check Gaming, Jazz Gaming, Kings Guard Gaming, Knicks Gaming, Lakers Gaming, Magic Gaming, Mavs Gaming, Nets GC, Pacers Gaming, Pistons GT, Raptors Uprising GC, T-Wolves Gaming, Warriors Gaming Squad, and Wizards District Gaming.

MHCG created NBA 2K product design concepts, leveraging urban streetwear themes, racing stripes, branded logo patterns, and more, with key high-touch details to establish the chairs as coveted collectors items. MHCG also devised a direct-to-consumer ecommerce rollout strategy with a custom league-driven website shopping experience and sponsored social media to generate hype around the collaboration. We worked with Raynor to showcase the NBA 2K League partnership and solidify its status as the official chair of Esports with a custom booth design for E3 — a premier trade event for the video game industry which drew a crowd of over 66,000 in 2019 alone.

In 2019, MHCG worked with Raynor Gaming to promote brand awareness through a suite of ads at the NHL Stanley Cup Playoffs. We have also worked closely with Raynor Gaming on presentations for leading retailers and consumer packaging.

projects.title: The Classic
projects.subtitle: Evolving attention to detail with an immersive specialty coffee brand based in LA.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development
projects.description:
In 2013, The Classic opened its first brick and mortar, high-end specialty coffee shop in downtown Los Angeles. As in many cities, coffee shops occur every fifteen feet in downtown L.A. — so our challenge was to differentiate The Classic brand and promote its reputation as a luxury fashion brand.

MHCG examined the existing visual system and quickly spotted an opportunity to transform and reposition The Classic to communicate quality as both a coffee retail space and a fashion powerhouse. Replacing a distorted, busy logo with a clean and distinctive brand identity, and implementing the visual system across multiple channels and touchpoints, The Classic’s fresh new look has made quite a statement in downtown L.A.; clean and polished visuals give the brand impact, and make it feel unique within the Fashion District. Featured frequently in Vogue, Harper’s Bazaar, and Elle, the redesign has been described as “trendy, modern, and fashionable — but always welcoming.”

projects.title: FXFL
projects.subtitle: Optimizing fandom for a professional football league.
projects.services: Digital + Web Development, Social Content
projects.description: It’s every fan’s dream — a football league that reaches out to its fan base, plays during the week, and contains its costs. Welcome to FXFL, a professional football league that doubles as a developmental platform for players, coaches, and referees alike. Launched in 2014, FXFL looked to MHCG to immerse fans fast and gain momentum by giving them access to the league across platforms. Using a WordPress content management system, MHCG created an engaging FXFL interface to build excitement for the new league. As FXFL built fans, we optimized interest by maintaining player profiles and team statistics on desktop and mobile — fully integrating the experience through social media and news service outlets.

projects.title: Fun & Yummy
projects.subtitle: Exploring the intersection of chocolate and augmented reality.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Social Content
projects.description:
Fun & Yummy is a confectionery experience that connects high-impact chocolate candy to a newly developed digital platform, enhancing and extending personal consumer experience. MHCG created a brand identity system for Fun & Yummy that positions it as the children’s and young adults’ candy of choice, merging friendly characters and a playful chocolate atmosphere into an integrated digital solution.

MHCG identified consumer data and patterns, and used them to apply intelligent digital innovations that support Fun & Yummy brand engagement through a fully-immersive brand-owned media channel. Interactive across tactile and digital platforms, key Fun & Yummy activation points help to bring the brand closer to its younger consumers, building a deeper understanding of the demographic and increasing direct sales through in-app purchases.

Based on the belief that kids grow through play, each Fun & Yummy package contains an activation point which unlocks various aspects of the Fun & Yummy AR mini-game.

Timing and skill-based challenges and puzzles in the Fun & Yummy AR video game hone motor skills while providing hours of fun, interactive entertainment.

projects.title: Milberg Factors
projects.subtitle: Leveraging decades of experience for one of the largest US factoring firms.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
Milberg Factors, Inc. is one of the largest factoring and commercial finance firms in the U.S. Headquartered in the heart of midtown Manhattan, they serve clients ranging from small, privately-held companies to publicly-traded corporations, varying in size from $5 million to over $500 million in sales — including manufacturers, wholesalers, and service providers.

MHCG worked with Milberg Factors to develop a brand identity reflective of their prominence in the industry, and of their 75 years of experience.

projects.title: PCCTC
projects.subtitle: Boosting development for a leading consortium for cancer research.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development
projects.description: The Prostate Cancer Clinical Trials Consortium (PCCTC) is a clinical research group specializing in phase I and phase II trials in prostate cancer, headquartered at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center. MHCG worked with PCCTC to develop their website, as well as on website development for one of their featured cancer trials, DORA.

projects.title: Israel at 70
projects.subtitle: Making history come to life to celebrate the State of Israel’s 70th birthday.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, Environments + Trade Shows
projects.description:
MHCG created the graphic identity and collaborated on the design of an environmental installation for an event to celebrate the historic 1947 United Nations vote that formally established the state of Israel, presented by the Israeli Mission to the UN. The event took place at the NYC-owned Queens Museum building — the historic site of the 1947 United Nations General Assembly — despite initial controversy surrounding the museum’s politically-sensitive decision to renege then reinstate its offer to host the event.

Israel at 70 featured United States Vice President Mike Pence as the keynote speaker and guest of honor, as well as speeches by Israeli Ambassador to the UN Danny Danon, United States Ambassador to Austria Ronald S. Lauder, Israeli Minister of Transportation and Intelligence Yisrael Katz, and President of the State of Israel Reuven Rivlin. It was broadcast live on American television networks.

MHCG branded the Israel at 70 event, and designed and produced invitations, a registration website, social media graphics, environmental signage, lanyards, VIP name badges, lapel pins, roll ups, and country placards. Designs featured a motif of the flag of Israel revealing the pivotal UN vote in 1947.
projects.description2: Israel at 70 featured United States Vice President Mike Pence as the keynote speaker and guest of honor, as well as speeches by prominent US and Israeli government officials. It was broadcast live on US television networks.

projects.title: Intelligence Options
projects.subtitle: Defining a trustworthy, value-driven intelligence brand.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print
projects.description:
Intelligence Options has a wide scope of capabilities. They provide risk solutions that help clients prevent, respond to and remediate risk, have an expertise in screening, investigations, and intelligence, and handle analytics and data management.

They needed a branding solution that respected the seriousness of what the company did as well as convey trustworthiness to potential clients. MHCG worked closely with them to develop the precise image they needed.

projects.title: Chai Lifeline
projects.subtitle: Rebranding a preeminent health support network for children with cancer.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
Chai Lifeline is a worldwide organization that originally provided support to cancer patients and survivors, and mushroomed into an umbrella organization providing over a dozen sub-brands and services for those facing tragedy.

MHCG helped Chai Lifeline explore a logo rebranding that recognized the power of their affiliated sub-brands (of which they were losing brand equity). The new logo is warm, inclusive and approachable, with uplifting shapes and colors indicative of a lifeline that welcomes everyone. The updated identity was vibrant, engaging and conveyed the new brand promise.  The new visual system provides a distinct but easy-to-use toolkit for creating communications and outreach materials.

projects.title: IES
projects.subtitle: Rebranding a structural transition for a leading national electrical contractor.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print
projects.description:
IES is a multi-faceted electrical contractor with a national presence in all areas of commercial and industrial construction. As a nationwide provider of electrical design, construction and maintenance services preparing to transition to a holding company, they came to MHCG to provide a unified look and feel to help them continue to expand in these fields.

We helped them explore an adaptable identity system appropriate for the transition, which gave them the flexibility for application across the brands and divisions in their portfolio.

projects.title: Newseum
projects.subtitle: Capturing the power of media manipulation to increase media literacy.
projects.services: Digital + Web Development, Social Content
projects.description:
The Newseum, an interactive museum in Washington D.C., promotes free expression and champions the five freedoms of the First Amendment through exhibits, public programs, and education initiatives. MHCG created the design, development, and content strategy for a series of Facebook-sponsored social ads to increase media literacy in the general public by learning to spot manipulative propaganda techniques used in digital advertising.

MHCG created a tongue-in-cheek visual narrative for the social campaign influenced by pop art of the 1960s. Content juxtaposed illustrative parodies of tactics used in media manipulation — such as incomplete facts or fear-mongering — with tips for how to stay aware, and drove traffic to the Newseum’s own education resources to learn more.

The campaign was backed against a seven-figure media budget in conjunction with a leading not-for-profit foundation.

projects.title: Parklee
projects.subtitle: Simplifying the experience of parking across New York City.
projects.services: Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content
projects.description: Parklee is a community-driven concept to help people find parking more easily. Drivers and parking space owners are encouraged to use Parklee to list their own private parking spot, and to find a parking space to rent. They connect drivers with empty parking spaces in their area in order to minimize driver frustration and wasted time and gas.

projects.title: Meridian Capital Group
projects.subtitle: Rebranding one of the nation’s leading real estate finance firms.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture
projects.description: Meridian Capital Group is widely-recognized as one of the country’s leading, prolific, commercial real estate finance and advisory firms. They turned to MHCG to revamp their brand identity to emphasize the honesty and integrity they provide to their investors, and to highlight the speed and reliability with which they operate.

projects.title: Estates at Alpine
projects.subtitle: Marketing the prestigious 60-acre former Frick estate in Alpine, New Jersey.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
Only 8 miles from Manhattan in Alpine, NJ, sits the historic family estate of Henry Clay Frick II, now known as the Estates at Alpine. The magnificent 60-acre parcel was reconceived and subdivided to preserve the main estate and create an additional twelve exceptional luxury estate sites for the area’s most prestigious homes. MHCG designed the brand identity, marketing campaign, and website for the Estates at Alpine.

Inspired by the Frick estate’s ultimate privacy and exclusivity, the brand identity mirrors the nestled estates and grand history. The wordmark is set in a glyphic revivalist serif, and while contemporary layouts and hairline sans serifs headlines ground the marketing in present day. MHCG designed an exquisitely-produced hardcover book to introduce the property, and an inviting, responsive website for digital marketing.

projects.title: Cory Booker 2020
projects.subtitle: Creating a visually distinct and ownable American flag identity to symbolize 2020 presidential candidate Cory Booker’s campaign.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture
projects.description:
Despite his position as a Senator in the United States Congress and bid to the American people for the highest seat in the nation, Senator Cory Booker from New Jersey did not wear a lapel pin. Our mandate was to create a visually identifiable, 100% distinct and ownable lapel pin to symbolize the 2020 presidential candidate’s campaign and reinforce his campaign promise of American unity.

MHCG developed a flexible, heartland-inspired brand identity to convey the movement and dynamic energy surrounding Booker’s forward-thinking democratic campaign ideas to unite the American people and build a more fair and just country. The round shape is organic to represent unity, and the dynamic, flowing stripes call forward a wave of revolution across the U.S.

Our work adapts the American flag design and offers a forward-thinking alternative to the traditional flag pin now worn most prominently by members of the Republican Party. The identity was designed to be worn by presidential candidate Senator Booker, staff, current and prospective donors, and campaign supporters, as well as sold as official licensed apparel through the campaign site.

Senator Cory Booker suspended his 2020 Democratic presidential campaign on January 13, 2020.

projects.title: Older Adults Technology Services
projects.subtitle: Powering an identity refresh for a nonprofit that’s transforming the way seniors interact with technology and the world.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print
projects.description:
Older Adults Technology Services (OATS) is a nonprofit organization that provides technology education and resources to help older adults navigate a wired world. Headquartered in New York City, with branch locations across the United States and programs that impact older adults around the globe, OATS also serves as a vital consulting resource for government agencies, nonprofits, and large corporations, such as Comcast, Capital One, AARP, MTA, and the New York Public Library, who turn to OATS to help them solve challenges in their industries related to aging and technology.

OATS turned to MHCG to give their branding a much-needed refresh, and redesign their website and corporate collateral. MHCG updated and carried through iconic power symbol in the OATS logo, to symbolize the power that technology has to make our everyday lives easier and the empowerment and dignity that learning to harness those tools gives to senior citizens. MHCG reinvigorated the outdated identity by adjusting the geometry of the identity system and introducing a vibrant new color system to bring OATS into the digital era.

projects.title: Mike Bloomberg NYC
projects.subtitle: Spearheading campaign materials for direct marketing to minority communities in NYC.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Print, Direct Marketing
projects.description: As Mayor of New York City from 2002–2013, Mike Bloomberg brought change and reform to the city in ways most New Yorkers could never have imagined. At MHCG, we’re proud so say we had a hand in shaping Bloomberg’s campaign, and the city we live and work in.

projects.title: Bank Hapoalim USA
projects.subtitle: Growing relationships with personalized attention for a boutique bank.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
Bank Hapoalim is a full-service commercial bank, providing expert financial solutions to businesses, business owners, and private banking customers. The bank was anxious to expand its share of the financial portfolio beyond the one or two services most clients used. MHCG redesigned supporting collateral material to emphasize the Bank’s true capabilities.

The results? A new energy among both BHI USA employees and clients as they expand the role of the bank in their business and personal lives.

projects.title: UX3
projects.subtitle: Launching brand identity and packaging for a line of nutritional supplements.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
John Rowley knows what it takes to be successful, especially in performance and health. With his patented UX3 — a high-performance dietary supplement and meal replacement formula — John was ready to take his new product line, Rowley Nutrition Systems, to the next level. He sought a partner to carve out a differentiated position for UX3 in a competitive, repetitive, and highly-saturated marketplace focused on health, nutrition, and wellness.

To find a niche for UX3 to thrive, MHCG conducted extensive management and consumer research interviews. We observed that UX3 and Rowley Nutrition Systems needed to better communicate their impressive credentials to an influential audience, starting with a distinctive logo and packaging. Communicating more than the product’s nutritional information and benefits, these branded elements provide a dramatic point-of-sale marketing tool.

As a Digital Agency of Record, MHCG aligned the creative with smart, digital brand positioning, implementing a comprehensive redesign of all UX3 packaging elements, email marketing, social media, and the e-commerce website. As its’ brand reputation grows, UX3 now makes an emotional connection with athletes, celebrities, and wellness-driven consumers looking to enhance their health and performance.

projects.title: Gilbert
projects.subtitle: Transforming the image of affordable Manhattan residential housing.
projects.services: Digital + Web Development, Social Content
projects.description: The Gilbert is a brand new Upper East Side mixed-income apartment complex of over 150 units, with picturesque views overlooking the East River. Complete with amenities, such as a mail room, bike storage, community lounge, fitness center, and landscaped garden with a play area for children, the Gilbert sits conveniently along the newly developed Q train at 96th street and 2nd Avenue. MHCG designed a social media marketing campaign to catch the attention of vibrant young professionals and families working in the area’s many schools and healthcare facilities.

projects.title: Belz Enterprises
projects.subtitle: Displaying a vision for adaptive, residential re-use developments.
projects.services: 3D Visualization
projects.description: MHCG concepted and produced 3D photorealistic renderings of The Citizen, a $33.5 million multi-family, adaptive re-use development in midtown Memphis, TN. The first phase of construction will convert the building into a $24-million mixed-use space with 173 apartments, parking, and ground-floor retail. Additions in the second phase of construction will include 26,500 square feet of commercial space. Construction began late in 2017.

projects.title: Political Campaigns
projects.subtitle: Extending reach for prominent politicians and political campaigns.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
We’ve been on every side of the election, fighting for both people and issues. Whether it’s a woman’s right to choose, marriage equality, or guns on our streets, MHCG crafts the most effective message and delivers it with a creative punch. We use all avenues — from traditional print and direct mail, to digital and social media campaigns that not only influence and inform opinions, but drive actions.

Politics is a tough game, and we love a good fight.

projects.title: Celadon Financial Group
projects.subtitle: Modernizing a boutique financial firm while preserving its legacy.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print
projects.description: Since 1986, Celadon has provided financial institutions, professional traders, and high-net-worth investors with advanced tools and high-touch broker-dealer services. Celadon looked to MHCG to help the company modernize its brand, marketing communications, and website while retaining the heritage and integrity of its image. Inspired by the financial company’s commitment to delivering evolving tactics in a dynamic marketplace, MHCG developed an energetic logo mark and type treatment with modular adaptations to brand its key service line, Celadon Prime®, and its philanthropic trading nonprofit, Celadon Foundation. MHCG developers also organized the company’s many service offerings into an easily navigable and responsive website.

projects.title: American Technion Society
projects.subtitle: Advancing awareness for critical, state-of-the-art research and scholarship.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Premiums + Promotions, Stakeholder Engagement
projects.description:
Based in New York City, the American Technion Society (ATS) provides critical support for visionary education and world-changing impact through the Technion-Israel Institute of Technology, ranked among the world’s leading science and technology universities. 

MHCG was lead design team and key brand drivers for high-end donor collateral and marketing materials for the opening of the Cornell Tech campus on New York’s Roosevelt Island. Our work included brand development for the kick-off Technion World Tour initiative, complete with total brand design, website landing page development, and the creation of all critical branded print collateral assets. Our work for ATS also included an unprecedented, highly-effective, and widely-distributed direct mail campaign.

projects.title: Yudayahito Sushi
projects.subtitle: Serving up a fresh brand identity system for a new-school sushi experience.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, Environments + Trade Shows
projects.description:
Yudayahito offers a different kind of sushi dining experience — prioritizing the highest quality ingredients and raw, craveable twists on the classic omakase approach. MHCG partnered with Yudayahito to translate this alternative sensibility into a fresh brand identity system, premium packaging concept, and integrated marketing strategy for the restaurant.

While our work with Yudayahito is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: Yeshiva University
projects.subtitle: Advancing tradition for Yeshiva University’s annual holiday celebration.
projects.services: Brand Identity + Architecture, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Environments + Trade Shows, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
Yeshiva University approached MHCG and partners to create a compelling, mission-driven brand and event concept for its 98th annual holiday dinner, celebrating its Rise Up fundraising campaign and the institution’s legacy of achievement. MHCG and partners developed a cutting-edge experiential event concept using augmented reality and immersive technologies to showcase the university’s dynamic global impact and fundraising initiatives.

Highlights include event branding and collateral, lighting design, environmental graphics, 3D signage installations, interactive touchscreen kiosks, pop-up theaters with surround-sound video, a state-of-the-art culinary experience, and student holograms welcoming guests to the event.

More than 500 friends and donors of the university were showed out in support, supercharging the university’s fundraising efforts and boosting the year-end total to a record-breaking $100 million. 

For more information about our work with Yeshiva University, please contact us.

projects.title: Amudim
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Print, Direct Marketing, Social Content
projects.description: For more information about our work with Amudim, please contact us.

projects.title: Boracho Hard Seltzer
projects.subtitle: Creating a brand identity and packaging system for a new hard seltzer company.
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Brand Identity + Architecture, Digital + Web Development, Direct Marketing, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions, 3D Visualization
projects.description: Boracho engaged MHCG to design an identity for its hard seltzers that would help the beverage stand out on the shelves in what has become a very crowded market. While our work with Boracho Hard Seltzer is confidential, we’d love to discuss how a similar scope of work can apply to your business. Please contact us for more information.

projects.title: STONE RIDGE HOLDINGS GROUP
projects.subtitle: Designing Clarity in Crisis for Israel's Global Emergency Syste
projects.services: Strategy + Transformation, Premiums + Promotions, Environments + Trade Shows, Stakeholder Engagement, 3D Visualization
projects.description:
MHCG partners with Stone Ridge Holdings Group to support the development of its physical brand expression across apparel, gifting, and event-based applications spanning its investment platform, including Stone Ridge Asset Management, NYDIG, LIFEX, and SRDAX.
Stone Ridge operates across true alternatives, energy investing, and bitcoin-focused strategies, among others — requiring a consistent but adaptable approach to how the brand is represented across institutional, cultural, and live environments.
MHCG designs and produces many of Stone Ridge’s corporate apparel programs, premium gifting, and curated swag kits deployed across key cultural and sporting moments, including the US Open and FIFA World Cup, as well as across industry conferences and trade shows.
In addition, MHCG provides support across Stone Ridge’s own hosted events, including its CEO/CIO Conference and Energy Conference, developing branded materials and physical touchpoints that extend the firm’s presence within its core investor and industry audiences.
At the experiential level, MHCG has supported activations that translate investment themes into participatory environments. At Future Proof Festival in Huntington Beach, this has included the LIFEX Longevity Challenge — pairing Stone Ridge’s longevity-focused investment thesis with a grip-strength bar-hanging installation, a widely recognized indicator of healthspan and longevity.
Across these touchpoints, our key focus is on translating complex investment narratives into clear, high-fidelity physical brand moments that align with Stone Ridge’s institutional positioning and areas of focus.

projects.title: What Would You Wish For?
projects.subtitle: Reimagining a world of heroes and opportunities.
projects.services: Digital + Web Development, Social Content, Premiums + Promotions
projects.description:
What Would You Wish For? is an imaginative, inspirational children’s book to benefit UNICEF USA, which helps children think about how they can change the world for good. Written by David Sable, Senior Advisor at WPP and formerly the Global CEO of Y&R, and illustrated by award-winning author, illustrator, and typographer, Emma Yarlett, the book helps children learn the potential their wishes — and their actions — have to make an impact on the world around them. The book was released in the spring of 2020, published by HarperCollins’ Zonderkidz and featuring an original song by Grammy-award winning artist and activist Peter Yarrow, of Peter, Paul and Mary.
MHCG developed an interactive webpage design for readers to learn more about the book and its contributors, as well as a comprehensive social media campaign to drive pre-orders and engagement.

masterplan.topItalic: We identify high-impact messaging and brand fixes you can implement within 24 hours.
